vulnerabilities.title: Buffer Overflow in parse_input()
vulnerabilities.description: Potential buffer overflow when copying user input without bounds checking
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use strncpy() or snprintf() with proper bounds checking

vulnerabilities.title: Use-After-Free in cleanup_session()
vulnerabilities.description: Memory is accessed after being freed in session cleanup
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Set pointer to NULL after free() or reorder operations

vulnerabilities.title: Integer Overflow in allocate_buffer()
vulnerabilities.description: Integer overflow in size calculation can lead to heap overflow
vulnerabilities.code_snippet: size_t total = count * item_size; // Potential overflow

vulnerabilities.title: Null Pointer Dereference in process_request()
vulnerabilities.description: Function parameter not validated before use
vulnerabilities.code_snippet: return request->type; // request could be NULL
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add null pointer check before dereferencing

vulnerabilities.title: Format String Vulnerability in log_error()
vulnerabilities.description: User-controlled format string in logging function

vulnerabilities.title: Double Free in error_cleanup()
vulnerabilities.description: Memory freed twice in error handling path
vulnerabilities.code_snippet: free(buffer); // Already freed in normal path
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Set pointer to NULL after first free or use single cleanup path

vulnerabilities.title: Memory Leak in create_connection()
vulnerabilities.description: Allocated memory not freed on error path
vulnerabilities.code_snippet: conn = malloc(sizeof(connection_t)); // Not freed on error
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Add proper cleanup in error handling paths

vulnerabilities.title: Race Condition in update_counter()
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use atomic operations or proper locking

vulnerabilities.title: Unused Variable in helper_function()
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove unused variable or mark as [[maybe_unused]]

vulnerabilities.title: Parameter Should Be Const in print_data()
vulnerabilities.description: Function parameter not modified but not marked const
vulnerabilities.code_snippet: void print_data(char* data) { // Should be const char*
